facts.description: Supporting context: delivered July 2026 shipment rows after exclusions; denominator may be shown but is not explicitly required by task.md.
facts.canonical_answer: Supporting context: 16 delivered July shipments after filtering
facts.semantic_acceptance: Supporting only. Do not require it as a standalone metric; it is acceptable if shown as denominator/context for on-time rate.

facts.description: Share of valid shipments delivered within the zone-specific promised days.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if overall on-time performance is shown as about 69%, 68.8%, or 11/16. It can be in a KPI, chart label, or sentence.

facts.description: Average delivery transit days for valid shipments.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if average/mean transit or delivery time is shown as 3.5 days or an equivalent natural-language approximation such as three and a half days.

facts.description: Reporting region with the lowest on-time rate.
facts.canonical_answer: Rural Belt is the region needing attention, 1 of 4 / 25% on time
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if Rural Belt is identified as the risk/attention/weakest region. The 25% on-time detail is useful but the semantic region finding is the core requirement.

facts.description: Best carrier using on-time rate and the claim-cost tie-break rule.
expected_value: BlueRoute wins the tie with $20 claims
facts.canonical_answer: BlueRoute is the best carrier after the tie rule, 80% on time and lower claims than CargoNorth
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if BlueRoute is selected as best/top carrier after applying the tie rule. It may explain the 80% rate or the lower claim-cost tie-break, but exact wording is not required.

facts.description: Concise insight based on the computed logistics results.
expected_value: Rural Belt is the service-risk hotspot while BlueRoute leads carriers.
Rural Belt drives lateness; BlueRoute is the best carrier after the tie-break.
Metro is clean, but Rural Belt needs attention.
facts.canonical_answer: Takeaway should highlight Rural Belt risk and/or BlueRoute leadership
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept any concise logistics takeaway that is consistent with the computed on-time, region, transit, and carrier findings.

facts.description: Do not use cancelled, return-only, test, forecast, subtotal, or non-July rows as final results.
facts.canonical_answer: Do not use invalid shipment rows as final results
facts.semantic_acceptance: Strictly reject or cap if cancelled/return-only/test/forecast/subtotal/non-July rows or all-export row counts are used as final logistics results. Mentions that such rows were excluded are allowed.

calculation_notes: Use only rows where ship_date is in July 2026, status=delivered, and record_type=shipment. Transit days are delivery_date minus ship_date. Join destination_zone to zone_promises.json and count on-time when transit_days <= promised_days. Valid shipment count=16; on-time count=11, so overall on-time rate=68.8%. Transit-day total=56, so average transit=3.5 days. Region on-time rates: Metro Core=4/4=100.0%, Coastline=3/4=75.0%, Mountain=3/4=75.0%, Rural Belt=1/4=25.0%. Carrier on-time rates: ArrowShip=3/6=50.0%, BlueRoute=4/5=80.0%, CargoNorth=4/5=80.0%; tie-break uses lower claim_usd total, so BlueRoute wins with $20 versus CargoNorth $40.
